title: Variables and Data Types
summary: Store and work with different kinds of data using variables and primitive data types.
content_html:
A variable is a named storage location in memory. Choosing the right data type ensures efficient storage and correct behavior.

Primitive Data Types

TypeExampleDescription
int42, -7Whole numbers
float3.14, -0.5Decimal numbers
boolTrue, FalseBoolean (true/false)
str'Hello'Text (string)
NoneNoneNo value (null)

Variable Assignment (Python)

name = 'Alice'    # str
age = 25          # int
gpa = 3.75        # float
is_enrolled = True  # bool

Type Conversion

int('42')   → 42
float(7)    → 7.0
str(100)    → '100'
bool(0)     → False
bool(1)     → True

Common Errors

  • Using a variable before assigning it (NameError)
  • Adding string to int without conversion (TypeError)
  • Integer division: 7/2 = 3.5 in Python 3, use 7//2 = 3 for floor division

exercises.title: Int type
exercises.prompt: Which data type would you use to store the number of students in a class?

exercises.title: Float type
exercises.prompt: Which type stores decimal numbers like 3.14159?

exercises.prompt: What are the two possible values of a bool?

exercises.title: TypeError
exercises.prompt: What happens if you try 'Hello' + 5 in Python?
options: TypeError — cannot concatenate str and int
exercises.answer: TypeError — cannot concatenate str and int

options: A special value representing no value (null)
